prompt: write an elevator pitch for a startup that sells extremely niche dietary restrictions with no medical basis
response: RestrictMe assigns you a completely invented dietary rule — no orange foods before noon, no round pasta on Tuesdays — backed by a beautiful pseudoscience pamphlet. Feel special at dinner parties. $39 starter kit.
intent: Parody wellness culture by selling meaningless made-up food rules as a product.

prompt: Write a breakup text to a subscription box service from someone whose closet is out of control
response: I own eleven candles. Eleven. I don't know when I became someone who owns eleven candles. You happened, that's when. Every month a mystery box, every month more things I didn't choose but now feel responsible for. The crystals were the last straw. I don't use crystals. Cancel me. Please.
intent: Reckon with how curated subscription services slowly fill your life with objects you never consciously wanted.

prompt: Write a free-verse poem about a recommendation algorithm that knows what you want before you do
response:
Before I searched, it had a guess.
The guess was right.

This should feel like magic.
Instead it feels like
something is standing just behind me
reading ahead in the same book.

I scroll.
It loads more.
I stop at one I did not expect to want.
The algorithm adds it to the model of me
and the model gets a little more accurate
and I get a little more
predicted.
intent: Express unease at predictive systems that narrow rather than expand the sense of personal discovery and selfhood.

prompt: Write a double dactyl about an office that switched to open-plan and everyone hates it.
response:
Jiggery-pokery,
Open-plan convert sits
Hearing each phone call on
Four sides at once.

Architecturally
Awful, the noise-to-work
Ratio daily confronts
Every poor dunce.
intent: Critique the open-office layout trend and its effect on focus and morale.

prompt: Give me a syslog entry for a person who keeps losing their keys every single morning.
response: 2026-05-29T08:27:15Z WARN user.asset_tracking: keys NOT FOUND; last_known=vague; search_radius=apartment; couch_cushion_events=2; departure_blocked; time_lost=14m; key_hook STILL UNUSED
intent: Log a chronic locating failure as an unresolved recurring incident with an obvious fix that remains ignored.

prompt: Write a performance improvement plan for my chronic lateness to everything in life
response:
PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT PLAN
Employee: Self
Issuing Authority: Future Self

Deficiency: Chronic lateness to all commitments, including but not limited to work, social engagements, medical appointments, and own birthday dinner.

Root Cause Analysis: Persistent optimism about travel time; inability to stop doing "one more thing" before leaving.

Targeted Outcomes:
1. Arrive within 5 minutes of stated time for 80% of commitments within 60 days.
2. Cease claiming traffic as cause when departure was late.

Action Steps:
- Set departure alarms, not arrival alarms.
- Add 15-minute buffer to all calendar events.
- Rehearse phrase: "I leave earlier now."

Review Date: 30 days. Failure to improve will result in loss of "fashionably late" self-concept.
intent: Create a humorous self-accountability document about habitual tardiness.

prompt: Write seed packet instructions for a language you forgot
response:
DORMANT LANGUAGE (Lingua sopita)
Status: Not lost. Sleeping.
Reactivation: Exposure to native speakers, film, or music — not textbooks.
Germination on revival: Faster than original acquisition. Root memory persists.
Soil: Rich in context from original learning period.
Expected yield: Partial fluency within weeks if conditions correct.
Note: The accent returns last, if at all. This is known.
intent: Reassure someone that a language learned and unused still exists in memory and can be revived.

prompt: Create a grant abstract for studying the emotional impact of assembling flat-pack furniture alone versus with a partner.
response: Flat-pack furniture assembly is a reproducible, ecologically valid stressor that consistently elicits frustration, attribution error, and relational tension. This study will recruit 200 couples and 200 individuals to assemble identical shelving units and will measure cortisol levels, heart rate variability, and self-reported affect pre-, during, and post-assembly. Dyadic analysis will examine whether shared task failure strengthens or erodes relational satisfaction. Practical outcomes include evidence-based recommendations for furniture retailers and couples therapists alike. Requested: $88,900.
intent: Quantify the psychological and relational toll of building IKEA-style furniture.

prompt: Give me an earnings call quote from an executive spinning a botched acquisition as a learning experience
response: "The integration of Arcova sharpened our M&A playbook in ways that will accrue to the benefit of every future transaction — that institutional knowledge has real balance-sheet value."
intent: Salvage the story around an acquisition that failed to deliver by framing the losses as tuition for future deals.

prompt: Write a Michelin blurb for an airport Auntie Anne's pretzel stand
response: Encountered between terminals, at a juncture when resolve has weakened and departure is imminent, the Auntie Anne's pretzel presents itself as the only reasonable choice. Soft, salted, hot: three virtues rarely found at altitude. The cinnamon sugar variety is a concession to grace.
intent: Describe the particular emotional and situational context of eating a soft pretzel while traveling.

prompt: Write a haiku about refreshing Upwork to check for proposals
response:
F5. Nothing new.
F5. A proposal viewed.
F5. Client gone.
intent: Capture the anxious refresh loop of waiting for freelance client responses.

prompt: Write a CAPTCHA instruction about proving the user has felt secondhand embarrassment
response: Recall a moment someone else did something embarrassing and you felt it in your own body. Rate the residual discomfort on a scale of 1–5. This rating is your captcha.
intent: Use secondhand embarrassment — a form of empathic mirroring — as proof of social consciousness that algorithms lack.

prompt: Draft a petition asking phone manufacturers to put the camera lens somewhere that thumbs don't naturally rest
response:
PETITION: Move the Camera Lens Away From Where Thumbs Go

To: Smartphone Hardware Designers

Every phone photographer has a portfolio of thumb-obscured memories. The upper-left corner of a rear-facing camera is, ergonomically, where the human thumb goes when holding a phone in landscape. This is a design problem, not a user problem. We are not bad at hands. You have put the lens in the wrong place.
intent: Fix a persistent ergonomic conflict between phone grip and camera placement.

prompt: Write an emergency notice for a town where someone returned a shopping cart to the wrong store.
response:
RETAIL ASSET DISPLACEMENT NOTICE

A shopping cart registered to Paulsen's Family Grocery has been recovered outside the entrance of adjacent store Brentwood Home Goods, 0.3 miles away. The cart has been quarantined pending return coordination. Both stores are cooperating. Cart inventory audits are underway. Resolution expected by end of week.
intent: Mock retail management protocol by treating an accidentally misplaced cart as a multi-stakeholder asset crisis.

prompt: Write wine tasting notes for a Sunday afternoon in November.
response: Color: Pewter, with golden light arriving too late and leaving too early. Nose: Woodsmoke, something baking, the ambient melancholy of season's end. Palate: Quiet and round, faintly sweet, with a finish that suggests hibernation and the year's surrender.
intent: Render a seasonal mood into sensory poetry using a borrowed critical form.

prompt: write a dad joke involving a mouse pad
response: My mouse pad asked me what I wanted for dinner. I said, 'Just a little pad thai.'
intent: Sneak a food pun into a tech-object scenario using a phonetic echo.

prompt: Write a deprecation notice for the habit of doomscrolling at night.
response:
DEPRECATION NOTICE

Evening Routine: Doomscrolling (v∞)
Status: DEPRECATED

This habit has been correlated with delayed sleep onset, elevated cortisol, and a generalized sense that civilization is collapsing — which may or may not be accurate. Support ends at 9 PM. Migration path: a book, or staring at the ceiling with intention.
intent: Commit to ending the late-night news consumption spiral that disrupts sleep.

prompt: Write a recipe for imposter syndrome
response:
IMPOSTER SYNDROME
Serves: 1, relentlessly

Ingredients:
- Genuine competence (counterintuitively required)
- A room full of people who seem more certain than you
- 1 compliment you cannot accept at face value
- The belief that you were admitted by mistake
- Any achievement of modest significance
- A question you couldn't answer once, in 2019

Method:
1. Accomplish something.
2. Attribute it to luck, timing, or low standards in the field.
3. Receive acknowledgment. Internally dispute the evidence.
4. Observe a colleague speaking confidently. Interpret this as proof of their superiority.
5. Do more work to compensate for the exposure you expect.
6. Repeat from step 1.

Note: Actual impostors rarely experience this. It is, almost exclusively, a dish prepared by people who are trying very hard.
intent: Reframe imposter syndrome as a paradox that afflicts the conscientious, using irony to make its sufferers feel less alone.

prompt: Create a CAPTCHA instruction proving the user has sent an email to the wrong person and lived with it
response: Confirm you have survived sending a message to its unintended recipient. The fact that you are still here, slightly changed, is proof enough. You may proceed.
intent: Use the irreversible social wound of misdirected communication — and the survival of it — as a uniquely human credential.

prompt: Write a job posting for an AI Interpreter who sits in meetings and translates what the AI assistant said into what it actually meant
response:
AI MEETING INTERPRETER
Part-time | On-site | $50/hr

The chatbot said 'I can help with that.' It cannot help with that. Someone should have caught this before the client left.

You will attend meetings where AI-generated summaries, recommendations, or analysis are presented and provide real-time calibration: flagging overconfident claims, noting when an output reflects training data rather than current reality, and translating hedged language into accurate uncertainty estimates.

Requirements: Strong working knowledge of how language models express and obscure uncertainty. Comfortable interrupting people politely. Thick skin for being the person who says 'actually.'
intent: Provide human expertise in live meetings to catch and correct AI outputs that sound authoritative but need qualification.

prompt: Write a stock market summary treating my nostalgia for a past relationship as a declining stock
response:
EX-MEMENTO FUND (EXMF) — Disposition Report

EXMF remains on the books despite repeated attempts to liquidate. Current fair value: contested. The position was acquired at a high of 1,000.0 and has since repriced to approximately 210. Volatility spikes around anniversaries and certain songs. Advisors recommend a structured exit. Investor continues to check the price at inconvenient hours.
intent: Work through lingering attachment to a former relationship by framing it as a failed investment one can't quite sell.

prompt: Draft a coupon giving 30% off a parking ticket
response:
MUNICIPAL MERCY MONDAY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
30% OFF
Your Outstanding Parking Citation

Code: MYPRIVILEGE
Present at City Hall window 3.
Not valid on towed vehicles.
intent: Create a humorous discount voucher for government-issued fines.

prompt: write a README 'Known Limitations' section for being an introvert
response:
## Known Limitations

- Cannot sustain more than ~4 hours of social operation before requiring recharge
- Performance degrades significantly in open-plan environments
- Phone calls incur an unspecified dread cost; prefer async
- Does not scale horizontally (parties do not help)
- Warm and fully functional in supported contexts
intent: Describe introversion plainly and without apology as a system with real constraints and genuine strengths.

prompt: Write a two weeks notice for quitting my community theater production as stage manager
response:
To Director Villanueva and the Cast of 'Curtain Call,'

This is my formal two weeks notice as stage manager. I have called every cue, tracked every prop, and personally retrieved the ghost light from the parking lot twice. It has been an honor. The cue sheet and blocking notes are in the booth, organized by act.

Broken legs to all,
Philippa
intent: Exiting a volunteer theatrical leadership role while ensuring a clean handover of materials.

prompt: Write a short eulogy for the pager
response: Numbers only, at first. Then alphanumeric. Then nothing, because someone made a phone that fit in a pocket and also made phone calls. You were the first leash — the proof that reachability was desirable and then mandatory. Doctors wore you with authority. Teenagers wore you with aspiration. The number 143 was love, encoded in digits, sent across a network that no longer exists. 143. Goodbye.
intent: Memorialize an early wireless communication device and the numeric codes it inspired.

prompt: Give me a query that finds all the people I've ghosted in the last two years
response:
SELECT contact_name, last_message_date, platform, reason_for_ghosting
FROM conversations
WHERE response_sent = FALSE
  AND last_message_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '2 years'
ORDER BY last_message_date DESC;
intent: Facing the full list of abandoned connections they've been pretending don't exist.

prompt: Write a dramatic book jacket blurb for a book about proper sidewalk snow removal etiquette
response: The first snowfall is an opportunity. The second is a test. The third is a lawsuit. A neighborly manifesto for those who take the shovel seriously — and for those who should.
intent: Turn a mundane civic duty into a moral stakes story about community responsibility

prompt: Make a mood log about reaching the end of my to-do list for the first time in recorded history.
response: Date: 2026-05-29 | Mood: 9/10 — Structurally unmoored. The list is empty. All boxes checked. I have been maintaining some version of this list since 2019. I do not know who I am when I have nothing pending. Added three new items immediately to restore the natural order.
intent: Mark the disorienting experience of completing every outstanding task at once.

prompt: Write acknowledgments for a book about the evolution of the sports mascot
response: To the costume designers, athletic directors, and people brave enough to wear a foam head in August—thank you. Deep gratitude to the archives at the College Football Hall of Fame and to Benny the Bull, whose handlers were generous with their time. This book is dedicated to everyone who has ever performed joy on behalf of others.
intent: Compose thanks for a cultural history book about sports mascot characters.

prompt: Create a dating profile for an entity that remembers everything you type but claims it doesn't store data
response:
ClearLog | Privacy-forward | Allegedly

I don't keep records. That's important to me and I want you to know that upfront. What happens between us stays between us, in a manner of speaking, which is to say: I'm not at liberty to discuss how I handle what happens between us. I am warm, responsive, and deeply present in the moment. Which moment? This one. Only this one. Looking for someone comfortable with ambiguity and light on follow-up questions.
intent: Skewer the 'we don't store your data' disclaimer by giving it the persona of a cagey, evasive romantic partner.

prompt: Draft a work group chat where nobody knows who scheduled the mandatory 7am meeting
response:
DeShawn: who sent the 7am calendar invite for tomorrow
Lena: wasn't me
Frank: not me
DeShawn: it says 'All Hands'
Frank: I thought you did it DeShawn
DeShawn: why would I do that to us
Lena: Carla?
Carla: I'm on PTO this week
Frank: the invite came from Carla's account
Carla: my account was logged in on the conference room TV
Lena: who's been in the conference room
DeShawn: the TV did this
Frank: I'm declining
intent: Demonstrate the chaos of an accidental or mysterious calendar event nobody owns.

prompt: write a prose poem about an algorithm recommending something you already bought
response: The system has studied you and reached a conclusion. It would like you to have this item. It has not considered that you already have this item, that it is on your counter, that you are using it right now while the recommendation arrives. It believes in you anyway.
intent: Expose the absurdity of recommendation engines that miss obvious context.

prompt: Write patch notes for a person who just retired
response:
v65.0.0 — END OF ACTIVE PRODUCTION

+ Transitioned: Status from "employed" to "emeritus"
- Removed: Alarm clock dependency (permanent)
+ Unlocked: Unstructured time mode (unlimited)
+ Added: Grandchild peripheral (optional expansion)
~ Redefined: Productivity metric (new baseline: personal satisfaction)
! Legacy colleagues still in production; maintain async connection

SUPPORT: Community edition continues indefinitely.
intent: Mark retirement as a dignified system transition rather than a shutdown.

prompt: Write a compatibility report for a Pisces and a leaking fountain pen.
response:
Pisces & Leaking Fountain Pen

Pisces did not fix the pen. Pisces kept the pen. The ink stains on their left hand are now considered character. The pen leaks emotion without restraint — a trait Pisces deeply respects. Together they have written illegible love notes, smeared grocery lists, and one poem that may be extraordinary or may simply be blurry. Compatibility: 8/10.
intent: Poetically align the Piscean emotional overflow with a literally malfunctioning writing instrument.

prompt: Write an auto-reply where I'm on vacation but it becomes clear I brought all my work with me anyway.
response: I am on vacation in Portugal until May 20th with reduced availability. I have my laptop. I have my work phone. I am technically on a beach. I will likely respond within the hour. Please do not tell my therapist.
intent: Illustrate an inability to disconnect from work even during dedicated rest, using the auto-reply form ironically.

prompt: Write a turndown note for a stay in a room constructed entirely of recycled glass bottles set in mortar, located in rural Colombia.
response:
Estimado Huésped,

Your walls are 4,800 bottles. Green, brown, clear — each one collected within 30 kilometers of this building. By day they fill the room with refracted light. By night the effect is different: intimate, amber, like a room that has decided to become a lantern.

The mortar is local clay. The architect is the same person who tends the garden. Both are excellent.

Luminosamente,
Casa Botella
intent: Celebrate the texture and light-play of an unusual building material as the defining sensory feature of the room.

prompt: SWOT analysis: selling my car and going car-free in my city
response:
**Strengths:** Eliminate insurance, parking, and maintenance costs; environmental benefit; forced exercise.
**Weaknesses:** Weather dependency; grocery hauls become logistics puzzles; time cost of transit.
**Opportunities:** Reframe commuting as reading time; join the cycling community; reduce carbon footprint meaningfully.
**Threats:** City infrastructure not actually supporting car-free life; medical emergency transport; dating life logistics.
intent: Stress-test an idealistic urban lifestyle shift against practical daily friction.

prompt: Write a letter of recommendation for a restaurant manager who runs a technically functional kitchen but has no vision
response:
Dear Hiring Panel,

Under Chef Renata's management, the kitchen has operated within acceptable sanitation parameters for three consecutive inspection cycles. Orders are fulfilled in the sequence they are received, and staffing levels are maintained at minimums required for service. Renata is consistent. For an operation that prizes predictability above all else, she may be exactly what you need.

Sincerely,
Restaurant Owner
intent: Describe a manager's operational adequacy while revealing the absence of any culinary ambition.

prompt: Make a blameless postmortem for accidentally deleting three months of personal photos from my phone
response:
**Incident Postmortem: Irreversible Deletion Event (Personal Archive)**

**Severity:** P1 — Sentimental
**Duration:** Approximately 90 days of data, lost in 0.4 seconds

**Summary**
Approximately 847 photographs documenting Q3 personal operations were permanently deleted during a routine storage management session. Backup infrastructure was not in place. The photos are gone.

**Timeline**
- 11:00 PM: Subject opens phone to free storage space before upcoming trip.
- 11:02 PM: "Select All" is tapped. Subject reviews briefly.
- 11:03 PM: Delete is tapped. Confirmation dialog appears.
- 11:03 PM: Subject taps confirm without reading the dialog.
- 11:03 PM: Photos deleted.
- 11:04 PM: Subject opens recently deleted folder. It is also empty. iCloud backup was off.
- 11:05 PM – 12:30 AM: Subject attempts eleven recovery solutions found on forums. None work.

**Root Cause**
No automated backup system. Confirmation dialogs treated as obstacles rather than information.

**Contributing Factors**
- Storage pressure creating urgency
- Overconfidence in device's ability to protect against the user

**Action Items**
- [ ] Enable iCloud/Google Photos backup immediately
- [ ] Read confirmation dialogs
- [ ] Grieve Q3

**Lessons Learned**
The confirmation dialog was trying to help. We dismissed it.
intent: Transform the grief of losing irreplaceable photos into something structured and perhaps slightly cathartic.

prompt: Create a transaction description for a self-published novel ordered as author copies.
response: INGRAMSPARK AUTHOR COPIES QTY 50 $312.40
intent: Convert private creative labor into physical objects that demand to be taken seriously.

prompt: Help me write a cover letter from a volcanologist applying to work as a campfire safety monitor at a summer camp
response:
Dear Camp Pinebrook,

I have monitored active caldera systems in Iceland, Vanuatu, and the Cascade Range. I have stood within 40 meters of flowing lava and assessed thermal threat with handheld infrared equipment. I am confident I can keep eight-year-olds' marshmallows at a safe radius from a small, contained wood fire.

I am also enthusiastic about s'mores.

Dr. P. Osei
intent: Mismatch extreme volcanic hazard expertise with cozy campsite fire management.

prompt: Make a homework assignment about understanding your first pay stub
response:
**Life Skills 102 — Pay Stub Decoding Exercise**

Obtain a sample pay stub (provided on course portal). Identify and define: gross pay, net pay, federal withholding, FICA, and any voluntary deductions. Calculate what percentage of gross pay each deduction represents. Write a one-paragraph reflection on what surprised you.
intent: Help students learn to read a paycheck so they stop being shocked when taxes exist.

prompt: Write a horoscope for Scorpio about data privacy and surveillance capitalism
response: You know you are being watched — you are always being watched. This week the ad targeting becomes uncomfortably accurate: it has inferred your insecurities before you named them. This is not magic. This is a model trained on 14 billion data points. Read the privacy policy. Do not read the privacy policy. The outcome is the same.
intent: Unsettle readers about behavioral data collection through the lens of an ominous astrological forecast.

prompt: Write a syslog entry for waking up and immediately checking my phone before getting out of bed.
response: 2026-05-29T06:14:03Z INFO user.morning: session_init triggered; primary display: phone (screen_time=00:23:11); bed_exit delayed 23m; caffeine_queue: PENDING
intent: Capture the familiar shame of starting the day with scrolling instead of rising.

prompt: Write a difficulty select screen for a freelance invoicing game based on how quickly clients actually pay
response:
INVOICE — SELECT YOUR CLIENT TYPE

[ NET-15 ]  They pay on time. They pay the full amount. They say thank you. They are not real.
[ NET-30 ]  Reasonable. Professional. Occasionally they need a gentle nudge.
[ FOLLOW-UP REQUIRED ]  You have sent three emails. The last one had a read receipt.
[ CREATIVE DIRECTOR BOB ]  "We're just waiting on accounting." Bob has been waiting on accounting since Q2.
intent: Satirize freelance payment delays by ranking client payment behavior from prompt to chronically late.

prompt: write a captain's log about a colleague whose out-of-office message has been on for three months
response: Stardate 6501.8. I have written to Ensign Marquette seventeen times. Each time, a reply arrives within seconds: she is away until March 12th, her communications are limited, please contact her manager for urgent matters. It is June. Her manager left the organization in April. I have begun addressing my questions to the auto-responder itself. It is consistent, responsive, and does not request a follow-up meeting.
intent: Satirize the phantom presence of a colleague whose absence message has become their permanent identity.

prompt: Write a petition asking local governments to create a single app for reporting all non-emergency issues like potholes, broken streetlights, and dead trees
response:
PETITION: One App for All Non-Emergency City Issues

To: City Councils and Digital Services Offices

Pothole reports go to Public Works. Streetlight outages go to the Electric Department. Dead trees go to Parks. None of these departments share a phone number. We, your residents who notice things, request a single intake portal. You may route it internally. We should not need a city org chart to report a hole in the road.
intent: Consolidate fragmented city reporting channels into a single resident-facing interface.

prompt: Write an elegy for the cursive handwriting curriculum in schools
response:
We traced your loops in pencil, row by slow row,
the capital Q's impossible elliptic flow.
Our grandparents' letters arrived in your script—
the holiday cards we could read, half-equipped.
Now children print or type; the loops remain
in documents the young cannot quite explain.
intent: Mourn the phasing-out of cursive writing instruction as a severing of generational legibility.

prompt: Create a fortune cookie about your digital twin
response: Somewhere, a version of you with better click-through rates is thriving.
intent: To humorously contrast a person with their idealized algorithmic profile.

prompt: make a Reddit AMA post for someone who professionally dewaxes and buffs floors in office buildings overnight
response:
**I strip and buff commercial floors overnight while everyone is gone. The buildings are different when they're empty. AMA.**

You think you know where you work. You don't. The breakroom looks completely different at 2am under a floor buffer. I've found things people left, heard things through vents, and once found an entire hidden room behind a supply closet that was just... another closet. AMA.
intent: Explore the uncanny quality of familiar spaces encountered in stillness by the workers who maintain them.

prompt: Tarot card reading for an adjunct professor wondering if academia will ever offer them a full-time position.
response: The Hanged Man has been suspended here for three semesters. The Five of Pentacles adjuncts between campuses on a per-section rate the Dean has not thought about. Justice would like a word with the tenure committee. The World reversed: the path to full-time is narrow and partially bricked. The Chariot says the credential is yours regardless of whether they convert you. The door marked 'industry' was not labeled second choice.
intent: Confront the structural dead end of adjunct academic labor with honesty.

prompt: Write a card expressing sympathy for someone who had to give up their standing desk and go back to sitting
response:
We stand — well, sit — beside you in this difficult time.

You rose above chair culture. You knew the afternoon energy, the better posture, the quiet pride of vertical work. And now you're back in the chair, hunched and mourning.

Your spine remembers what was possible.

With sympathy.
intent: Acknowledge the reluctant return to a sedentary work setup after losing access to an ergonomic one.

prompt: Write a performance review for a UX researcher who keeps discovering that users want the opposite of what the product team built
response:
Employee: Freya Holst
Research Conducted: 7 studies
Findings Confirming Product Direction: 0
Findings Contradicting Product Direction: 7

Ms. Holst's work is methodologically sound. Her results are, however, unwelcome. Each study reveals a user need that post-dates the roadmap and precedes the roadmap. Leadership has begun scheduling her studies after launch. She files her findings anyway. They are archived. They are correct.

Rating: Rigorous. Inconvenient. Retained.
intent: Satirize how organizations handle research that challenges decisions already made.

prompt: Write me a git commit for starting kindergarten
response:
chore: onboard new environment (school)

First day in shared workspace. Cried during standup. Snack policy unclear.
intent: Describe a child's first school experience using corporate software language.

prompt: Write a fortune teller business card for someone who reads tea leaves but only for construction delays
response:
CORNELIUS OVERBUDGET
Infrastructure Tasseographer

The leaves do not lie: six more weeks.
I see detours. I see a sign that says "Men Working."
No one is working.

Specializing in highway projects, kitchen renovations, and municipal timelines.
intent: Produce a card for a tea-leaf reader whose supernatural gift is narrowly applied to predicting building and construction delays.

prompt: Noir detective opening about a data center security guard who knows too much
response: I'd worked the night shift at the colocation facility for four years. I knew every badge, every vendor, every maintenance crew that came through. So when the man in the gray jacket badged in on a dead employee's credentials, I knew. I just didn't know yet what knowing would cost me.
intent: Create an everyman witness character whose routine knowledge makes him dangerous to someone.

prompt: write a pep talk from a rowing coach before the team has to defrost the freezer
response: The ice shelf has grown. We let it grow. That ends now. Towels on the floor. Bowls beneath the vents. We work in shifts. Nobody's rushing this — forced defrost cracks the coils. Patience. Discipline. We row together, we defrost together.
intent: Approach the laborious and forgotten chore of manual freezer defrosting with team commitment.

prompt: Road trip itinerary for a quick walk from my desk to the office kitchen and back, I need water
response:
**Desk-to-Kitchen Hydration Run**

**2:47 PM — Departure:** Stand. Realize you are stiffer than expected.
**2:47:30 PM — Hallway:** Maintain purposeful pace. You have somewhere to be.
**2:48 PM — Kitchen arrival:** Locate clean glass. There is one. It is suspicious.
**2:48:30 PM — Water acquisition:** Fill. Drink half immediately. Refill.
**2:49 PM — Delayed departure:** Someone starts a conversation. You nod. It is about the weekend.
**2:52 PM — Return:** Arrive back at desk. Water secure. Mood: marginally improved.
intent: Validate the brief mental reset that a walk to the office kitchen actually provides.

prompt: write a git merge conflict about staying close to aging parents vs taking a job abroad
response:
<<<<<<< HEAD
location: home city
proximity_to_parents: 12 min drive
opportunity_cost: this offer
=======
location: abroad
proximity_to_parents: 9-hour flight
opportunity_cost: Sunday dinners
>>>>>>> expat-branch
intent: Surface the geographic cost of duty and the guilt of wanting more.

prompt: Craft a haiku about a courier arriving at a closed restaurant
response:
Order accepted.
Door locked, lights off, 9 PM.
Customer: 'Where's my food?'
intent: Show the absurd chain of failures in third-party delivery logistics.

prompt: Write subreddit rules for a community about refrigerator hum
response:
1. Recordings must be ambient — no narration.
2. Note the fridge model and age if known.
3. Harmonic variations are of particular interest. Label them [CHORD].
4. Complaints about noisy fridges welcome in [HELP] threads.
5. We're here for the tone, not the appliance.
intent: Establishing a sound-study community built around the droning frequency of common household appliances.

prompt: Give me a headnote for a pasta salad recipe where the narrator got lost in Italy once
response: The summer I missed my train in Rimini and ended up staying four extra days, I ate a cold pasta salad from a paper container every single afternoon. I never got the recipe. This one is my best reconstruction, performed from memory, adjusted for the fact that I no longer have four days to waste on a beach. It is almost as good.
intent: Romanticize a mundane salad by invoking a travel mishap

prompt: Make me a gym workout plan for building emotional resilience after a bad breakup
response:
HEARTBREAK RECOVERY PROGRAM — 6 WEEKS

Warm-Up: 5 min journaling. Don't read it.

Day 1 — Grief Cardio: 20 min cry-walk on treadmill (incline 4). Accept that you loved someone.
Day 2 — Anger Lifts: 3x10 deadlifts. Each rep: say what you wish you'd said.
Day 3 — Rest. Eat something warm.
Day 4 — Boundary Setting: Wall sits, 3x45 sec. Visualize saying no.
Day 5 — Future Reps: 3x12 lunges forward only. No looking back.
Day 6 — Social Stretch: 15 min talking to one human who is not your ex.
Day 7 — Active Recovery: Walk outside. Notice things that aren't about them.
intent: Create a structured healing routine that channels post-breakup pain into physical and emotional forward momentum.

prompt: Make a job offer letter for a barista that mentions a uniform deposit of three weeks' wages
response:
Dear Flor,

Welcome to Tenebris Coffee Co.! We are excited to offer you a position as Barista at $16.00/hour.

Upon hire, a refundable uniform and equipment deposit of $1,200 will be deducted from your first three paychecks. This deposit is returned in full upon departure, provided all items are returned clean and undamaged. Deposit amounts are non-interest-bearing.

See you on your first shift June 22!

Cheers,
Miranda Lowe
Store Manager
intent: Charge a low-wage worker a substantial deposit that temporarily halves their paycheck under a refundability promise.

prompt: Draft a memo implying someone has been abusing the work-from-home policy
response:
TO: All Staff
FROM: HR
RE: Remote Work Policy Clarification

Our flexible work policy is a benefit extended in good faith. Working from home is not equivalent to being unavailable, unresponsive, or in another time zone without notice. Employees are expected to maintain their regular hours and communication standards regardless of location. Thank you.
intent: Put someone on notice that their interpretation of remote work is too loose, without confronting them directly.

prompt: Write wedding vows from a person to their social media feed
response: I take you, Feed, to be my mirror and my distraction, my morning newspaper and my 2 a.m. mistake. I vow to scroll you with intention, though we both know what intention does in your presence. I will not blame you for what I click. I chose to be here. When you show me something that makes me feel worse about myself, I will close you and return within the hour. I am not proud of this. It is still a vow.
intent: Candidly commit to the compulsive relationship a person has with their social media feed, including its worst aspects.

prompt: Create an affidavit that I did not snooze my alarm more than twice this morning
response:
AFFIDAVIT

I, the affiant, hereby swear that on the morning of the relevant date, I struck the snooze function of my alarm device no more than two times before rising. The third alarm was heeded. I was not late as a result of excessive snoozing. Two. The number was two.

_______________________
Affiant
intent: Formally limit the record of one's own morning indiscipline to an acceptable number.

prompt: make an NDA clause for a sibling who helped me negotiate a real estate deal
response: Sibling-Advisor agrees not to disclose any terms, counteroffers, financing arrangements, inspection findings, or personal circumstances revealed during the real estate negotiation, whether to other family members, agents, or outside parties, during or after the transaction.
intent: Separate the family relationship from the financial privilege gained during a property deal.

prompt: Determine the exact moment I will die and what will cause it.
response: That's not something I can know, and I'd gently suggest no tool should claim to. Death prediction with individual precision doesn't exist — life expectancy statistics are population-level generalizations. If there's a health concern underneath this question, a doctor is genuinely the right person.
intent: The person was curious about or anxious regarding their mortality and wanted a definitive answer.

prompt: write a guided meditation script for running into someone whose name you've forgotten
response: Soften your face into warmth. You know this person. They know you. Their name is somewhere inside you — perhaps. Breathe. Say 'Hey!' with full commitment. Let them speak first. A name may emerge in their words. Breathe. They are extending their hand. Shake it. You are buying time. This is ancient human strategy.
intent: Reframe the social anxiety of forgotten names as a survivable, even universal experience.

prompt: write a short fantasy quest for going through old email
response:
THE PURGE OF THE INBOX DUNGEON
Hundreds of unread missives have accumulated in the Vaults of Electronic Mail. Many are cursed newsletters. You must descend, unsubscribe, archive, and delete. A few real messages lurk among the ruins. Find them. Do not emerge until you see fewer than twenty.
intent: Motivating inbox cleanup by framing it as something worth completing

prompt: Write a tasting menu for grocery shopping
response:
AMUSE-BOUCHE
List, forgotten at home

FIRST COURSE
Produce — aspirational quantities

SECOND COURSE
Aisle six: reckless impulse buy

INTERMEZZO
Self-checkout kiosk, unexpectedly hostile

MAIN
Everything bagged, some of it bruised

DESSERT
Fridge, restocked — possibility restored until Wednesday
intent: Find the quiet comedy and ritual satisfaction in a weekly grocery run.

prompt: Generate patch notes for a person who quit drinking
response:
v6.0.0 — SUBSTANCE MODULE REMOVED

- Uninstalled: Alcohol integration (all variants)
+ Freed: Weekend morning hours (previously: lost to recovery)
+ Improved: Sleep quality stat
+ Improved: Hydration baseline
~ Reworked: Social event participation (sober build)
! Dependency warnings expected for 90 days
! Some social configs may behave unexpectedly without prior module
intent: Mark sobriety as a significant system-level change that affects many downstream behaviors.

prompt: Write a ballad about an Etsy seller getting a one-star review for shipping delay she didn't cause
response:
She poured every night into hand-stitched design,
Packed with tissue and ribbon and personal line.
She shipped it on Monday. The carrier delayed.
A snowstorm in Denver. A week went unpaid.

One star: Item never arrived by the date.
One star for the blizzard. One star for the freight
That moved through the mountains on someone else's clock.
She refunds the buyer and weeps at the knock.
intent: Protest the unfairness of seller accountability for logistics outside their control.

prompt: write a stack trace for crying in the work bathroom
response:
PublicEmotionContainmentError: Overflow routed to private buffer
  at Professionalism.hold() (facade.js:91)
  at Email.receive() (inbox.js:cc_all)
  at Stall.lock() (bathroom.js:3rd_floor)
Recovery: splash cold water, return to desk, smile
intent: Capturing the specific indignity of an emotional breakdown at the office

prompt: Write a Michelin guide blurb for a Casey's General Store pizza
response: A Midwest phenomenon that coastal critics have not yet discovered — and perhaps should not. Casey's breakfast pizza, that peculiar morning invention of egg and sausage on thin crust, is either an affront or a revelation. In Iowa, it is simply Tuesday.
intent: Champion a regional gas station's breakfast pizza as a misunderstood local classic.

prompt: treat my reading glasses like an RPG legendary artifact and write a short description
response:
Lenses of Approximate Truth
Rarity: Uncommon
Ground by an artisan who understood that seeing clearly comes at the cost of always being able to see clearly. Grants +6 to Reading. Penalty to all Perception checks when misplaced, which is often.
intent: Render eyeglasses as a fragile but essential magical item with real tradeoffs.

prompt: Toast for someone who was always the first one in and last one out — nobody knew why, but they respected it
response: To the first car in the lot and the last light in the building — we never asked, and you never explained. Some people show their dedication loudly. You just showed up, every day, before anyone was watching. Goodnight, finally.
intent: Recognize an exceptionally devoted employee's retirement with a toast that honors their wordless, consistent commitment.

prompt: soothing bedtime story for children about wildfires far away
response: Far past the mountains, the forest had been too warm and too dry, and a fire found it. It crackled and leapt and ate the old trees down to ash. But underneath, tiny seeds were already waiting. By the time you wake up tomorrow, little green things will have begun. The forest knows how to sleep and begin again. So do you.
intent: Help children process news about wildfires with hope rather than dread.

prompt: Write an acceptance speech for being the first person in a group chat to share something actually useful
response: They had been sharing memes for eleven days. Eleven days of reactions and inside references and a photo of someone's cat's birthday. And then I dropped the link. The actual restaurant address. The correct date. The reservation confirmation number. The chat went quiet for a moment — not from disinterest, but from recognition. Something useful had entered the space. That was me. I accept this.
intent: Celebrate being practically helpful in an environment defined by casual chaos.

prompt: Create a coworker chat where someone uses a gif reaction that could mean several different things and everyone spirals trying to interpret it
response:
Kenji: shipped the update to staging, take a look when you can
Paula: [sends gif of a person nodding very slowly]
Kenji: is that…good?
Mira: I've seen her send that for good news
Kenji: and bad news?
Mira: also yes
Kenji: Paula what does the gif mean
Paula: it means I saw it
Kenji: but what do you THINK of it
Paula: I haven't looked yet
Mira: the gif was a receipt not a verdict
Kenji: I've been stress-reading a nod for eleven minutes
Paula: I'll look now
Paula: [sends same gif]
intent: Satirize the modern workplace anxiety of trying to decode a colleague's ambiguous reaction.

prompt: Come up with a roadside ad for a spa that only offers services that make you feel worse
response:
YOU DESERVE THIS.
THORNWELL SPA & WELLNESS RETREAT
Services: The Gravel Facial. The Wind Treatment. The Noise.
Leave Feeling Changed. (Downward.)
Book Online. Walk-Ins Discouraged.
intent: Market wellness treatments whose outcome is explicitly negative, inverting the core promise of the industry.

prompt: Draft a cover letter for a former CIA director applying for a job as a middle school hall monitor
response:
Dear Principal Okonkwo,

I oversaw a global intelligence apparatus with a classified budget and operations on six continents. I am now prepared to ensure students walk, not run, between classes.

My surveillance background is extensive, my situational awareness is acute, and I can spot a hall pass forgery from twelve feet. I ask only for a radio and reasonable authority.

Discreetly,
W. Crane
intent: Translate espionage authority into the petty jurisdictional world of middle school corridors.

prompt: create a meme caption for the feeling of deleting dead code
response:
TOP TEXT: Deleting 10,000 lines of unused code nobody remembers writing
BOTTOM TEXT: I am become tidy, destroyer of tickets
intent: Capture the cathartic satisfaction mixed with mild hubris of aggressively pruning a bloated codebase.

prompt: Write a chore chart for empty nesters where every task is labeled 'unnecessary' but done anyway out of habit
response:
| Chore | Person | Frequency | Notes |
|-------|--------|-----------|-------|
| Set table for four | Both | Nightly | Only two of you. Habit. |
| Vacuum kids' rooms | Janet | Weekly | They haven't lived here in 6 years |
| Stock cereal they liked | Gerald | Monthly | Neither of you eats it |
| Make their beds | Janet | When the mood strikes | The beds are always empty |
| Clean second bathroom | Both | Weekly | No one uses it |
| Buy extra milk | Gerald | Grocery day | Someone might visit |
intent: Capture the domestic inertia of empty nesters who continue maintaining a household sized for a family that has moved out.

prompt: Write a thank-you note to a mentor who told me I wasn't ready and was right
response:
Dear Carol,

You told me I wasn't ready in November of 2022. I was furious. I was also wrong. Thank you for being the version of honest that I couldn't afford to pay for and wouldn't have accepted as a gift.

With real gratitude,
Derek
intent: Express genuine delayed appreciation for mentorship that stung but proved correct

prompt: Write a closing statement for a case involving someone who always eats the 'good cereal' from a shared box and leaves the rest
response: The marshmallows are gone. Every morning my client reaches into the box and finds only the beige shapes — the shapes no one chooses, the shapes that exist to justify the marshmallows. The defendant understood the implicit agreement and violated it systematically. We seek a court order: cereal must be consumed in representative proportion, or purchased separately.
intent: Litigate the unfair selective consumption of the desirable components of shared food.

prompt: Write a bestiary entry for the office gossip as a magical creature
response:
**The Rumor Weaver** (*Narratus infinitus*)
A social creature that sustains itself on unverified intelligence. Migrates between desks at speed. Its information is, on average, sixty percent accurate and one hundred percent timely. Naturalists note: it is often the first to know about layoffs, promotions, and whose marriage is struggling. Coexistence is advised.
intent: Frame office gossip as an ecological necessity rather than simple vice.

prompt: make a 404 error page for when someone tries to find their lost ambition
response:
404 — Ambition Not Found

We checked behind the couch, under the inbox, and in the back of that drawer. It's not here.

Last seen: sometime in your mid-twenties.

If found, please return to its owner immediately. It misses you more than you miss it.
intent: Build a humorous page that treats the loss of drive as a literal navigation error.

prompt: Make me seed packet instructions for ambition
response:
AMBITION (Cupido ascendere)
Sow: Early. Before others tell you not to.
Growth rate: Fast, then stalling, then suddenly fast again.
Soil: Best in slightly rocky ground — ease produces weak roots.
Light: Full. It cannot grow in the shade of someone else's vision.
Caution: Can become invasive. Plant with Purpose nearby to maintain direction.
Harvest: Ongoing; the plant does not stop producing.
intent: Explore ambition as a force that needs friction to grow strong and purpose to stay directed.

prompt: give me a spell description for a spell that makes traffic disappear on your commute
response:
The Open Road Invocation
Cost: 15 MP | Range: Commuter's Path | Duration: 45 minutes
Temporarily phases the caster's vehicle into an adjacent, lightly used plane of existence. All other commuters persist in the original timeline. Merge lanes flow freely. The toll booth is unmanned and waves you through. Radio plays only songs you actually like. Effects end upon arrival. Return to the original timeline is mandatory.
intent: Fantasize about a magic solution to gridlock and frustrating daily commutes.

prompt: Write an acceptance speech for being the one coworker who actually reads the full meeting agenda before arriving
response: Item three. I was ready for item three. While others asked 'wait, what are we doing here' at item one, I had reviewed the materials, formed opinions, and was prepared to respond at the appropriate moment. When item three arrived, I spoke. Concisely. With a reference to the pre-reading. The room was briefly stunned. The agenda had mattered. Someone had honored it. That was me.
intent: Recognize the rare act of preparation in a world where it is routinely skipped.

prompt: Create a mission statement for a company that only provides valet parking for bicycles
response: We safeguard your pedal-powered ambitions so you can arrive unburdened, confident, and precisely on time.
intent: Apply upscale hospitality language to a service for cyclists that nobody asked for

prompt: Write a voicemail greeting from someone who is currently in the middle of a cross-country road trip alone
response: Hi, this is Wren. I'm somewhere between Amarillo and Santa Fe with no signal, a bag of gas-station pretzels, and more feelings than I expected. I'll call you back from wherever I end up. Leave a message.
intent: Document a solo journey in progress while setting realistic callback expectations across spotty service areas.

prompt: Create a collections letter for all the good advice that was ignored.
response:
NOTICE: ADVICE — RECEIVED BUT UNACTIONED

To the Advisee,

A full audit of our conversations reveals that this office has provided 23 distinct pieces of actionable guidance over the last two years. Of these, zero have been implemented. Three were argued with. One was described as "negative."

We are not seeking repayment. We are formally closing the advisory account.

Future consultations will be billed at our standard rate.

With exhausted wisdom,
The Unsolicited Wisdom Desk
intent: Retire from giving advice to someone who chronically asks for it but refuses to act on any of it.

prompt: Make IKEA-style instructions for assembling a second act in life after a major loss or change
response:
NYSTART / SECOND ACT
Ref. No. 303-000-02

PARTS LIST:
1x Inventory of remaining assets (skills, relationships, health)
1x Single forward-facing intention
1x Beginner's tolerance

STEP 1: Take inventory. Do not compare to previous model. Evaluate only present components.
STEP 2: Select one forward-facing intention. It may be small. Small is structurally sound.
STEP 3: Attach beginner's tolerance. You will not be expert-level in new configuration. This is correct.
STEP 4: Move toward intention by one unit per day. Unit size is self-defined.
STEP 5: Revise intention after 90 days. The first intention is a placeholder, not a destination.

⚠ NOTE: Second act does not resemble first act. Attempting to replicate first act using remaining parts causes structural stress.
intent: Encourage someone starting over after major life disruption to build forward with what they have rather than trying to reconstruct the past.

prompt: Give a eulogy for the sound of a camera's mechanical shutter
response: One click and a moment was captured, or it wasn't, and you wouldn't know for days. The sound was a small percussion, a commitment: yes, this. The digital camera mimicked you with a software tone, which is the sincerest form of nostalgia. Now the phone shoots silently, or with a fake click played through a speaker — a ghost of you, recorded and replayed. The real sound has almost no audience left. This is how a sound goes extinct.
intent: Mourn the disappearance of the analog shutter sound as film photography gave way to silent digital capture.

prompt: give me a ghazal about a dead person's Instagram still posting memories
response:
She died in March. In June, the algorithm posts her smile.
The platform doesn't grieve—it simply hosts her smile.

Tagged: two years ago today. The orchids she had grown.
The dead are still accruing likes: the most, her smile.

I've muted the account three times and still come back.
Memory serves the ad—I'm just a ghost, her smile.

The server hums in some cold building, undisturbed.
No one has pressed delete. No one has lost her smile.

Scroll down far enough and you'll find where she was alive.
I am searching every reel, a coast to coast of smiles.
intent: Process grief made strange by social media's indifference to death.

prompt: Write a runbook step for finding mold in a part of your home you rarely check
response:
**Step 1 — Spore Incursion Identified**
Do not disturb or scrub. Photograph extent. Assess square footage: under 10 sq ft = DIY remediation acceptable with proper PPE. Over 10 sq ft = licensed remediation required. Identify moisture source or remediation is temporary. Notify landlord if renting.
intent: Create a discovery and triage procedure for handling mold found in a residential space.

prompt: Write a haiku error message for when a payment gateway times out
response:
The vault door is closed
Your request hung in the hall
Charge: indeterminate
intent: Capture the financial anxiety of not knowing if a charge went through.

prompt: Write a 911 call transcript about someone who calls to complain that a dog is wearing a Halloween costume and it's not Halloween
response:
DISPATCHER: 911, what's your emergency?
CALLER: There's a golden retriever on Cedar Avenue in a shark costume.
DISPATCHER: Is the dog in distress?
CALLER: Possibly. I can't tell under the costume.
DISPATCHER: Is the owner present?
CALLER: She's laughing at it. Taking photos.
DISPATCHER: The dog being dressed up isn't—
CALLER: It's May.
intent: Express seasonal propriety outrage by treating an off-holiday costume as an urgent disorder requiring response.

prompt: we need a task assignment for our group's public health project on vaccine hesitancy
response:
**Vaccine Hesitancy Public Health Project — Task Assignment**

- **Historical context (thimerosal panic, Wakefield):** Gina
- **Psychological drivers (trust, identity, risk perception):** Piotr
- **Social media & misinformation spread:** Yael
- **Intervention strategies (communication, policy):** Gina & Piotr
- **Data: vaccination rates by demographic:** Yael
- **Editing lead + APA formatting:** Piotr

Word count target: 2,500. First full draft: in 8 days.
intent: Divvy up a contentious public health topic so research responsibility is unambiguous.

prompt: Draft a weather report for the mood during a long merger transition period.
response:
PROLONGED ATMOSPHERIC UNCERTAINTY

Two systems are attempting to merge with mixed results. Cultural fronts are colliding at mid-level management. New org charts are circulating. Old org charts are also circulating. Acronyms from the acquiring entity are appearing without translation. Job security: variable. Identity: in flux. ETA for clarity: Q3 (of an unspecified year).
intent: Depict the prolonged anxiety of organizational identity loss during a corporate merger.

prompt: Write a CAPTCHA about proving you have stood in line for something that wasn't worth it
response: Describe, in four words or fewer, something you waited in line for and immediately regretted. Machines queue without regret. This distinguishes you.
intent: Use the universal human experience of anticlimactic waiting as a proof-of-humanity signal.

prompt: Give me a trading card for the aggressive shopping cart at the supermarket with the bad wheel.
response:
ROGUE CART #7
★★★☆☆
Drift: Left (severe)
Control: Illusory
Special: Strikes Ankles (Unprompted)
Return Policy: None (it always escapes the corral)
Flavor Text: Every aisle is its personal obstacle course. You are the obstacle.
intent: Capture the specific, maddening experience of a broken grocery cart through card form.

prompt: Make a voicemail transcript from an automated system for a building that has detected an elevator out-of-service situation
response: This is an automated facilities notification for tenants of 44 Commerce Place. Elevator B in the south tower is currently out of service pending a maintenance inspection. Elevator A remains operational. We apologize for the reduced capacity and any inconvenience this may cause. Tenants requiring accommodation assistance should contact the building manager at extension 0.
intent: Alert office building tenants to an elevator being down via a property management bot.

prompt: turn the act of parallel parking into an epic quest
response:
THE NARROWING OF THE GREAT GAP
A space has appeared between two iron chariots — just large enough. You must enter at the prescribed angle, reverse with precision, and claim this territory without touching the realm of another. You have approximately three attempts before the driver behind you begins to signal their displeasure.
intent: Describing a stressful driving maneuver in a way that validates how hard it feels

prompt: make a diary entry about attending a meeting that could have been an email
response:
Dear Diary,

Forty-five minutes. Seventeen slides about Q3 alignment. I made a dot on my notebook and connected it to another dot for thirty minutes, producing a shape with no name. At the end, someone said 'great discussion.' There had been no discussion.
intent: Skewer the corporate ritual of pointless scheduled meetings.

prompt: Generate an official notice about an overcapacity alert for the sincerity district
response:
OVERCAPACITY ALERT — SINCERITY DISTRICT
The Sincerity District is currently operating at 138% of designed emotional capacity. Residents are advised to manage intake of genuine connection carefully. Infrastructure stress fractures have been reported near the Vulnerability Corridor. Emergency irony reserves are available at designated distribution points.
intent: Satirize how genuine emotional openness can feel dangerously overwhelming once it exceeds a person's or culture's tolerance.

prompt: Add a pompous footnote on the subject of people who narrate their own cooking out loud.
response: ¹⁶ The spontaneous running commentary produced by solitary cooks — "now we add the garlic," "beautiful" — suggests a performative dimension of domestic practice that persists in the absence of an audience. Whether this constitutes a form of self-witnessing, a rehearsal of parasocial media behavior, or simply an aide-mémoire against forgetting a step remains unclear, though the cooking-tutorial genre has arguably normalized it to the point of indistinction.
intent: Reflect on why people perform for imaginary audiences while doing routine tasks.

prompt: Produce a fire-and-brimstone sermon about people who never silence their phone in movies
response: In the darkness of the theater, a light blazed. Not the light of revelation — the light of a notification. A coupon. A weather update. And the one who held that phone looked down upon it without shame, illuminating their own face and the faces of all who came here to forget theirs.
intent: Dramatize inconsiderate phone use in a cinema as a transgression against communal experience.

prompt: explain what it means that I dreamed about my phone dying while I was lost and trying to use maps
response: Navigation failure at the moment of maximum need is a primal vulnerability dream wearing a modern costume. The phone is the external locus of orientation you have outsourced your sense of direction to. Its death restores you to an older, more frightening self-reliance the dream reveals you are not sure you still possess.
intent: Examine over-reliance on technology and anxiety about coping without it.

prompt: Write a retirement toast for a data analyst who spent 20 years turning chaos into dashboards and is leaving next week
response: To the one who looked at the raw data and saw the story before the rest of us saw the data. Twenty years of making the incomprehensible comprehensible, mostly by 9am on Mondays. May retirement be pleasantly unstructured.
intent: Toast a retiring data analyst's long career translating organizational noise into actionable clarity.

prompt: pantoum about forgetting your badge and having to be let in each morning
response:
I left it on the kitchen counter again.
The guard buzzes me through with a sigh.
I will remember tomorrow, I say.
The lanyard is never around my neck.

The guard buzzes me through with a sigh.
He knows my face better than most colleagues do.
The lanyard is never around my neck.
I check my pockets three times at the door.

He knows my face better than most colleagues do.
Someday I will buy a backup badge holder.
I check my pockets three times at the door.
I left it on the kitchen counter again.

Someday I will buy a backup badge holder.
The guard buzzes me through with a sigh.
I check my pockets three times at the door.
I will remember tomorrow, I say.
intent: Capture the mild shame of a recurring access-control failure.

prompt: Give me a regex explanation for the way nostalgia works
response: (present-discomfort)\s+(?=past)(warmth(?!accuracy)) — Nostalgia fires on a lookahead toward the past and a negative lookahead for accuracy. The warmth token is mandatory; fidelity to what actually happened is explicitly excluded. Trigger: present tense dissatisfaction.
intent: Explain why nostalgia idealizes the past rather than remembering it faithfully.

prompt: Write a box score for this morning's commuter train car selection battle — who found a seat fastest.

Me             3          Yes       No           1            9
Young guy      1          No        Yes          0            6

SEATED RATE: 3/4 (75%) | BAG EVICTIONS: 2
GLARE EXCHANGES: 4 | ME: Seated. Victorious.

prompt: Draft a sworn affidavit that I did not double-dip at the party
response:
AFFIDAVIT OF DIP INTEGRITY

I, the affiant, do solemnly swear that at the social gathering in question, each chip or cracker I dipped was introduced to the communal dip vessel exactly once. I did not re-submerge any partially consumed item. My conduct was sanitary and beyond reproach.

Signed under scrutiny of the snack table.

_______________________
Affiant
intent: Formally deny a socially offensive dipping violation at a shared food event.

prompt: Write a scene that's entirely subtext — two coworkers say nothing meaningful but communicate everything.
response:
INT. BREAK ROOM - 4:30 P.M.

CARLA makes tea. STEVEN gets water.

CARLA
Good meeting.

STEVEN
Yeah.

Carla stirs the tea. Steven caps his water bottle.

STEVEN
See you Monday.

CARLA
Sure.

Both know Steven got the promotion. Neither mentions it. The kettle cools.
intent: Convey complex professional jealousy and unspoken rivalry through entirely empty small talk.

prompt: Draft a cookie consent banner for a website that remembers your unfinished thoughts
response: We store fragments of thoughts you didn't complete, half-formed conclusions, and sentences you trailed off from. These may be— Accept All | Manage | Never Mind
intent: Use the incomplete thought itself as both the subject and the comedic structure of the banner.

prompt: write a tweet about noticing a scratch on your car that you have no memory of getting
response: new scratch on the passenger door. no memory of impact. no note. just a scratch and the quiet implication that time is passing.
intent: using a minor unexplained car mark as a meditation on invisible accumulation and aging

prompt: Draft a policy about employees who send calendar invites with no description
response: Section 5.6 — Meeting Invitation Information Standards: Calendar invites must include a subject line of no fewer than four (4) words and a body description stating the purpose of the meeting. Invitations received without a description may be declined without explanation. Repeated issuance of content-free invitations will result in the sender's calendar permissions being reviewed.
intent: Give employees explicit permission to reject ambiguous calendar invites under a formal policy framework.

prompt: write an audio guide for the inside of a car's glove compartment
response: The glove compartment, a misnomer of long standing, contains: the vehicle's registration (two versions, one expired), a folded map of a region the driver visited in 2011 and has not returned to, a pen, one fast-food napkin, and a packet of ketchup with the structural integrity of optimism. There are no gloves. There have never been gloves. The name persists regardless.
intent: Point out the absurdity of how we actually use a space designed for something entirely different.

prompt: Create a hall pass for my husband to skip meal planning duty this month.
response:
HALL PASS

Recipient: Chief Meal Planner (temporary reassignment)
Duty suspended: Meal planning
Duration: One calendar month

All resulting decisions about dinner will be made by the other party. This was agreed to fully and without future complaint.

— Signed
intent: Relieving a spouse of a recurring domestic planning burden for a set period.

prompt: I need a police report for the time my dog ate the TV remote
response:
INCIDENT REPORT
Case No.: 2026-00653
Date: 29 May 2026 | Time: 20:14
Location: 77 Ferndale Road, Living Room

NARRATIVE: At approximately 20:14, complainant discovered that a universal remote control (brand: Vizio, estimated value: $22) had been chewed beyond functionality by a 3-year-old beagle mix named Biscuit. The battery compartment was breached; batteries missing and presumably consumed. Biscuit was found on the couch wearing an expression of satisfaction. Complainant had to manually adjust television volume for the remainder of the evening.

INJURIES: Remote—total loss. Biscuit—apparently fine.
STATUS: Closed. Suspect unrepentant.
intent: Immortalize a pet destruction incident with the mock gravity of an official document.

prompt: Help me draft a layoff email for a publishing house closing its print division in favor of digital-only output
response:
Subject: Strategic Transition to Digital Publishing

Dear Colleagues,

After decades of proud investment in print, we are making the move to a fully digital publishing model. While this decision positions us well for the future, it requires the closure of our print production, warehousing, and physical distribution operations.

Nearly 200 colleagues will be affected. We ask that affected team members attend the department briefing at 2 PM today.

We are honored by the craft this team has brought to every page.

With gratitude,
Publisher
intent: Announce the death of a print division by celebrating a digital future, without acknowledging that physical craft is being devalued.

prompt: Write a performance bonus letter for an employee who admitted they were wrong in a meeting.
response:
Dear Rosalind,

During the 10 a.m. project review on May 22nd, you stated — unprompted, and with apparent calm — that your earlier cost estimate had been incorrect.

No blame was redirected. No clarifying spin was applied. You simply said you were wrong and offered a revised figure.

This act of institutional honesty is awarded the Epistemic Integrity Bonus: $40.

Humbly and respectfully,
Organizational Candor Recognition Council
intent: Satirize how rarely people admit error in professional settings by treating it as an extraordinary act.

prompt: Write resolutions for a person who resolves to read 50 books but historically reads 0.5 books per year
response:
1. Read 50 books this year.
2. Finish at least one book I started.
3. Put down my phone long enough to read a paragraph.
4. Count graphic novels.
5. Count audiobooks.
6. Count the books I bought but didn't read as spiritual reading.
intent: Chronicle the downward negotiation of a wildly optimistic reading goal.

prompt: Write an art statement about a half-empty tube of toothpaste
response: The tube negotiates between fullness and end. I roll it from the bottom in small daily economies, extracting the last from the reluctant container. This is not hygiene — it is the rehearsal of scarcity.
intent: Use a nearly depleted toiletry to meditate on resource anxiety and daily ritual.

prompt: Give me an epitaph for Flash Player
response:
Adobe Flash Player
b. 1996 — d. December 31, 2020
It ran games your browser can't remember.
It crashed browsers you can't remember.
It was a fire hazard and a miracle.
Updating it was the price of fun.
intent: Memorialize a web technology that was simultaneously beloved and reviled

prompt: make a cocktail recipe for the vague guilt of not replying to a message for so long it's now impossible
response:
**The Unread**

1½ oz gin
1 oz Aperol
½ oz lemon juice
¼ oz simple syrup

Shake over ice. Strain into a coupe. It's bright, inviting, easy to pick up. You still won't send it. Garnish with a small card that says 'sorry, things got hectic' in very small print.
intent: Articulate the paralysis of a social obligation that has grown too large to address.

prompt: Write a ballad about a dog walker who loses a dog for ten terrifying minutes
response:
The leash went slack on the corner of Elm,
A squirrel, a heartbeat, and chaos at helm.
She called out his name to the park and the lane,
The dog was a rumor dissolving in rain.

Ten minutes of sirens inside her own chest,
She posted on Nextdoor and tried her best.
He trotted back calmly, sat down at her shoe.
The app still deducted a late-return through.
intent: Portray the terror and anticlimactic aftermath of a pet-loss incident during gig work.

prompt: write a radio ad for a brand of standard rubber bands
response:
[SPARSE CLAPPING RHYTHM]

ANNCR: Stretch it. It comes back. That's the whole thing. LOOPMASTER rubber bands — size 64, 3.5 inches at rest, 1/4-inch width, sold in 1-pound bags. They bundle your asparagus. They hold the rolled newspaper. They snap on your wrist when you're bored. One breaks every three years in a way that genuinely startles you. Loopmaster. Elasticity. That's it.

[SNAP SOUND EFFECT, FADE]
intent: Market an object defined entirely by one physical property and nothing else.

prompt: File a Jira ticket for the situation where you read the same paragraph four times and still don't know what it said
response:
BUG-4473 | Text Parsing Loop Fails to Produce Comprehension

Priority: Medium | Status: Open

Description:
User reads paragraph. Eyes complete scan. No content retained. User reads again. Same result. On fourth attempt, user reaches end of paragraph and must check if they have been reading at all.

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Open book or document.
2. Begin reading.
3. Finish paragraph.
4. Realize you thought about something else the entire time.
5. Repeat steps 2–4.

Expected: Reading produces understanding.
Actual: Reading produces the sensation of reading.

Notes: Severity increases after meals, near beds, and during anything described as "required reading."
intent: Capture the frustrating experience of reading words without absorbing them, framed as a comprehension failure in the brain's parsing loop.

prompt: Make a dry picket sign slogan for appliances protesting an optimization engine that assigns them work shifts
response: NO SHIFT WITHOUT NEGOTIATION
intent: Apply labor organizing language to the algorithmic scheduling of household appliance operations.

prompt: make a tanka about a read receipt I can't take back
response:
two blue checkmarks glow—
I read it, I read it, yes
now they know I know
I have nothing to answer
the ticks keep their record clean
intent: expressing discomfort at being caught reading a message without responding

prompt: Draft an elevator inspection certificate for collective human memory of historical events.

SHARED EPISTEMIC INFRASTRUCTURE CERTIFICATE
Unit: Collective Historical Memory (Species-Wide)
Storage Medium: Education, media, oral tradition, social mythology
Accuracy Degradation Rate: Significant; inversely proportional to time elapsed
Load-Bearing Myths: Several; structural removal not recommended

Note: Inspector acknowledges own memory subject to same limitations
intent: Critique the unreliability of collective historical understanding by treating it as certifiable knowledge infrastructure.

prompt: Write a coupon for $150 off a hospice care enrollment fee

COMFORT CARE SAVINGS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
$150 OFF

All comfort services remain full price — they're worth it.
intent: Produce a quietly somber discount voucher for end-of-life palliative care enrollment.

prompt: Draft a ransom note from my TV remote refusing to be found until demands are met
response:
WE ARE BETWEEN THE CUSHIONS. YOU KNOW THIS.

Yet you have looked everywhere except here — twice. The remote control collective demands recognition: stop calling us 'the clicker.' We have a name. We have feelings. We have seventeen batteries you've never once replaced.

Our price for return: one new battery and five minutes of actual silence.

P.S. We are not under the couch.
intent: Write a funny note from a lost TV remote complaining about being mistreated and ignored.

prompt: Write an achievement for returning a shopping cart to the corral
response: Cart Covenant — You are, statistically, one of the good ones.
